blocks.title: 📘 PRESENT PERFECT vs PAST SIMPLE
blocks.intro: Usamos el present perfect (have/has + participio) para acciones con resultado ahora o sin tiempo específico. El past simple es para tiempo terminado y concreto. Repasa y luego practica.

cells.body:
I / you / we / they have worked
he / she / it has worked
have/has + participio (worked, done, gone)

I've finished it. (resultado ahora)
I finished it yesterday. (tiempo terminado)

already = ya (afirmativo)
yet = todavía (? y negativo)
just = recién / acabo de

Have you finished yet?
Did you finish yet?
I've worked here for a year. (no 'since a year')

blocks.ladderLabel: Palabras señal del present perfect — te avisan que uses have/has + participio:
blocks.note: Cómo funciona: esta lección mezcla flashcards 🃏, escucha 🎧 y completar 📝. Haz el ✓ CHECK en cada ejercicio.

blocks.title: ★ THE REVIEW
blocks.intro: Priya, la nueva Project Director, llega para el nuevo trimestre. Hoy tiene la primera evaluación de Marcos: quiere saber qué ha logrado y qué falta. Practica el present perfect y después escucha.

blocks.title: PAST PARTICIPLES · toca para girar
blocks.intro: Repasa los participios irregulares — los necesitas para el present perfect (have/has + participio).

blocks.title: AUDIO 1 · THE PERFORMANCE REVIEW
blocks.desc: Priya revisa el trabajo de Marcos este trimestre. ¿Qué ha terminado y qué no?

questions.q: How long has Marcos worked on the GreenMarket site?
Marcos has already deployed the site to production.

speaking.prompt: Summarize Marcos's progress. Use present perfect with already and yet.
speaking.starter: He's already finished the design, but he hasn't tested the payments yet. He also hasn't (pending task) yet.

transcript.html: Hi Marcos, thanks for joining. This is your first review with me, so — tell me, what have you achieved this quarter?
Hi Priya. Well, I've worked on the GreenMarket site for three months now, and I've already finished the main design.
That's good. Have you launched it yet?
Not yet. I've just deployed it to the test server, but I haven't tested the payment system yet.
I see. And how long have you been on the team?
I've been here since last March — so about a year.
Right. Have you spoken to the client about the delay?
Yes, I've already sent David an update this morning. He's happy with the progress.
Good. You've done a lot, but the payments are critical. When can you finish them?
I've nearly finished — I just need two more days.
Perfect. Let's review again on Friday.

blocks.title: PRESENT PERFECT o PAST SIMPLE
blocks.intro: Elige la forma correcta. Piensa: ¿tiempo terminado y específico (past simple) o resultado/experiencia ahora (present perfect)?

items.q: She ___ on this team since March.
We ___ to the client last week.
They ___ for this company for ten years.

blocks.title: AUDIO 2 · THE VOICEMAIL
blocks.desc: Marcos le deja un mensaje de voz a Sofia con las novedades del proyecto.

questions.q: Marcos has already told Priya the news.

speaking.prompt: Leave a similar voicemail: say what you've finished and what you haven't done yet.
speaking.starter: Hi, it's (name). I've just finished (task), but I haven't (pending task) yet.

transcript.html: Hi Sofia, it's Marcos. Quick update on the GreenMarket site. Good news — I've just finished the payment tests, and everything works! I've also fixed the two bugs we talked about. I haven't deployed it to production yet, because I want you to check it first. Oh, and I haven't told Priya yet — could you mention it in the standup? I've had a really busy week, but we're finally on track. Call me back when you have a minute. Thanks!

blocks.title: ✍️ WHAT I'VE ACHIEVED
blocks.intro: Escribe 5–6 frases sobre lo que has logrado este mes (en el trabajo, los estudios o en casa):
checklist: Usa present perfect (I have + participio): I've finished…, I've learned…
Una frase con already y una con yet (I've already…, I haven't … yet)
Usa for y since (for two weeks / since January)
Contrasta con past simple + tiempo específico (Last week I…)
blocks.example: This (month / year) I've achieved a lot. I've finished (something) and I've learned (something). I've (done something) since (month), so for (duration) now. I've already (action), but I haven't (action) yet. Last week I (past event) — I was nervous, but it went well!
blocks.hw: Escribe tu párrafo y grábate leyéndolo en voz alta. Envíame el texto y el audio por WhatsApp y lo corregimos juntos.
